mission.title: kubestellar: bump peter-evans/repository-dispatch from 3.0.0 to 4.0.1
mission.description: bump peter-evans/repository-dispatch from 3.0.0 to 4.0.1. This issue affects 1+ users.

steps.title: Identify kubestellar feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubestellar deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=kubestellar
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubestellar -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: bump peter-evans/repository-dispatch from 3.0.0 to 4.0.1

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubestellar resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
Bump peter-evans/repository-dispatch from 3.0.0 to 4.0.1 in the GitHub Actions reverse map (`.gha-reversemap.yml`) and the workflows that use it.

steps.title: Apply the fix for bump peter-evans/repository-dispatch from 3.0.0 to 4.0.1
steps.description:
## Summary

Updates `peter-evans/repository-dispatch` GitHub Action from v3.0.0 to v4.0.1 (SHA `28959ce8df70de7be546dd1250a005dd32156697`).

**Changes:**
- Updated `.gha-reversemap.yml` with v4.0.1 SHA and tag references
- Updated `.github/workflows/goreleaser.yml` to use new SHA with version comment

## Related issue(s)

Issue #3677

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Upgrade kubestellar to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade kubestellar:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kubestellar jetstack/kubestellar --namespace cert-manager
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager
```

steps.title: Confirm bump peter-evans/repository-dispatch from 3.0.0 to is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubestellar -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.
